intro: Python's dynamic nature facilitates structural fluidity and fast iterations via scripting. Moving to C# introduces a rigid, statically typed component model backed by Microsoft's .NET ecosystem, optimizing for enterprise architecture and heavy multi-threaded workloads. Developers port workloads here to gain superior compile-time validation, explicit object-oriented design patterns, and native async/await efficiency at scale.

diffs.to: Static, strong, safe, with explicit and inferred typing (var).

diffs.from: asyncio event loop or multiprocessing to bypass GIL.
diffs.to: Task Parallel Library (TPL) with true multi-threaded async/await execution.
Compiled to Common Intermediate Language (CIL), JIT-compiled by CLR.
Brace-delimited blocks, mandatory class structures, explicit access modifiers.

examples.title: Async HTTP Request
examples.description: Fetching JSON text from a remote API asynchronously.
examples.fromCode:
import asyncio
import aiohttp

async def fetch():
    async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
        async with session.get("https://api.example.com") as resp:
            return await resp.text()

examples.title: List Filter and Transform
examples.description: Filtering an array and transforming the results into a new sequence.
examples.fromCode:
items = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
filtered = [x * 2 for x in items if x > 2]

tips.title: Leverage LINQ
tips.body: Replace Python list comprehensions with C# LINQ syntax (Where, Select) for readable data manipulation.

tips.title: Declare Access Modifiers
tips.body: Explicitly specify public, private, or protected for class members, as C# defaults to private.

tips.title: Utilize Structs vs Classes
tips.body: Use structs for small, immutable value types and classes for reference types to optimize allocations.

tips.title: Wrap I/O in Using Blocks
tips.body: Always use the "using" statement or declaration for IDisposable resources like streams and database connections.
